recap: Added --fix flag to python-lint: runs ruff format then ruff check --fix then ruff check, with bats tests covering fixable and unfixable scenarios.
Added --fix flag to zsh-lint: calls zshfix then runs normal lint check; violations surviving auto-format are reported with exit non-zero.

Created python-test bin script wrapping pytest, pytest install script mirroring ruff pattern, pyproject.toml for kitty config with pythonpath and testpaths, and bats tests covering passing file, failing file, and directory scenarios.

Created python-writer skill with SKILL.md (four-step TDD workflow), references/testing.md (pytest conventions, conftest.py, sys.modules stub, parametrize), and references/style.md (return early with before/after example).

Updated zsh-writer Step 4 to use zsh-lint --fix instead of zsh-lint, and split the blanket Python NEVER rule into two: one forbidding Python for new scripts, one directing python-writer when Python is explicitly requested.
Renamed zshfix → zsh-fix with stdout-by-default/--in-place/stdin pattern; updated zsh-lint to call zsh-fix --in-place; replaced NeoVim shfmt_zsh formatter with oroshi_zsh_fix using stdin=true.
